articles.title: Bright lights and hot orbs: UFO files shed light on sightings but leave interpretation to the public
articles.summary: On May 8, 2026 the US Department of War released its first batch of declassified UAP records, launching the public archive PURSUE, which the Trump administration says will be updated every few weeks. The release follows a February 19, 2026 directive from President Trump instructing federal agencies to identify, review and declassify documents related to extraterrestrial life and UAP, and includes dozens of agencies and tens of millions of records, many only on paper.

en.title: Bright lights and hot orbs: UFO files shed light on sightings but leave interpretation to the public
en.summary: On May 8, 2026 the US Department of War released its first batch of declassified UAP records, launching the public archive PURSUE, which the Trump administration says will be updated every few weeks. The release follows a February 19, 2026 directive from President Trump instructing federal agencies to identify, review and declassify documents related to extraterrestrial life and UAP, and includes dozens of agencies and tens of millions of records, many only on paper.

de.title: Helle Lichter und heiße Kugeln: UFO-Akten geben Aufschluss über Sichtungen, überlassen die Interpretation jedoch der Öffentlichkeit
de.summary: Am 8. Mai 2026 veröffentlichte das US-Kriegsministerium seinen ersten Stapel freigegebener UAP-Datensätze und startete damit das öffentliche Archiv PURSUE, das nach Angaben der Trump-Regierung alle paar Wochen aktualisiert wird. Die Veröffentlichung folgt einer Anweisung von Präsident Trump vom 19. Februar 2026, die Bundesbehörden anweist, Dokumente im Zusammenhang mit außerirdischem Leben und UAP zu identifizieren, zu überprüfen und freizugeben. Sie umfasst Dutzende von Behörden und Dutzende Millionen Aufzeichnungen, viele davon nur auf Papier.

articles.title: How to watch 'Sleeping Dog' online – stream controversial UAP/UFO doc from anywhere
articles.summary: Sleeping Dog, a documentary directed by Michael Lazovsky, follows journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell as he investigates UFO and UAP phenomena. The film draws on Corbell's personal archive of hundreds of hours of footage, including material from well‑known UFO figures such as George Knapp, John Lear and Bob Lazar. Lazovsky says the documentary serves as insurance for Corbell, who faces threats from rogue organizations and companies that allegedly try to silence him.

de.title: So schauen Sie sich „Sleeping Dog“ online an – streamen Sie die umstrittene UAP/UFO-Dokumentation von überall aus
de.summary: „Sleeping Dog“, ein Dokumentarfilm von Michael Lazovsky, begleitet den Journalisten und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell bei seiner Untersuchung von UFO- und UAP-Phänomenen. Der Film stützt sich auf Corbells persönliches Archiv mit Hunderten von Stunden Filmmaterial, darunter Material von bekannten UFO-Persönlichkeiten wie George Knapp, John Lear und Bob Lazar. Laut Lazovsky dient der Dokumentarfilm als Versicherung für Corbell, der Drohungen von Schurkenorganisationen und Unternehmen ausgesetzt ist, die angeblich versuchen, ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen.

de.summary: Der israelisch-britische Illusionist Uri Geller, 79, reagierte auf die Warnung des US-Kongressabgeordneten Tim Burchett, dass die nächste Ladung freigegebener UFO-Dateien die bereits freigegebenen in den Schatten stellen werde, und sagte, er glaube jedes Wort der Warnung. articles.title: A small Oregon town puts on a big UFO festival. Why it's worth a visit
articles.summary: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17, 2026, and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. The festival celebrates the 1950 UFO sighting by Paul and Evelyn Trent, whose photographs of a metallic object sparked national debate and are considered among the most famous UFO images.

de.title: Eine kleine Stadt in Oregon veranstaltet ein großes UFO-Festival. Warum es einen Besuch wert ist
de.summary: Das 26. jährliche McMenamins UFO-Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, findet vom 14. bis 17. Mai 2026 statt und wird vom McMenamins Hotel Oregon ausgerichtet. Damit ist es nach Roswell das zweitgrößte UFO-Festival in den Vereinigten Staaten. Das Festival feiert die UFO-Sichtung von Paul und Evelyn Trent im Jahr 1950, deren Fotografien eines metallischen Objekts eine landesweite Debatte auslösten und zu den berühmtesten UFO-Bildern zählen.

articles.title: Off-Beat: Federal agents, an Eye of Sauron, and what's really in the Pentagon's first UAP drop
articles.summary: S. Department of War posted 161 declassified UAP records to its PURSUE website after a February 19 Trump administration directive to open federal archives on unidentified aerial phenomena. Among the files is a 13‑page slide deck titled “Western US Event,” summarizing reports from seven federal law‑enforcement special agents (USPER1‑USPER7) who observed four distinct UAP incidents over two days in 2023 in the western United States.

en.summary: S. Department of War posted 161 declassified UAP records to its PURSUE website after a February 19 Trump administration directive to open federal archives on unidentified aerial phenomena. de.title: Ausgefallen: Bundesagenten, ein Auge Saurons und was wirklich im ersten UAP-Abwurf des Pentagons enthalten ist
de.summary: Das US-Kriegsministerium veröffentlichte 161 freigegebene UAP-Aufzeichnungen auf seiner PURSUE-Website, nachdem die Trump-Regierung am 19. Februar angeordnet hatte, Bundesarchive zu nicht identifizierten Luftphänomenen zu öffnen. Unter den Akten befindet sich ein 13-seitiges Dia-Deck mit dem Titel „Western US Event“, das Berichte von sieben Spezialagenten der Strafverfolgungsbehörden des Bundes (USPER1–USPER7) zusammenfasst, die im Jahr 2023 an zwei Tagen vier verschiedene UAP-Vorfälle im Westen der USA beobachteten.

articles.title: UFO files | In pursuit of pies in the sky
articles.summary: S. Department of War released 162 declassified documents, photos and videos on unidentified anomalous phenomena under President Donald Trump, with further releases planned through the PURSUE program. The release involves the White House, NASA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence and the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, and includes footage of UAPs over Greece, Iraq, the UAE and a football‑shaped object near Japan.

de.title: UFO-Dateien | Auf der Suche nach Kuchen im Himmel
de.summary: Das US-Kriegsministerium veröffentlichte unter Präsident Donald Trump 162 freigegebene Dokumente, Fotos und Videos zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen. Weitere Veröffentlichungen sind im Rahmen des PURSUE-Programms geplant. An der Veröffentlichung sind das Weiße Haus, die NASA, das FBI, das Büro des Direktors des Nationalen Geheimdienstes und das All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office beteiligt. Sie umfasst Aufnahmen von UAPs über Griechenland, dem Irak, den Vereinigten Arabischen Emiraten und einem fußballförmigen Objekt in der Nähe von Japan.

summaries.file-release-bright-lights-and-hot-orbs-: On May 8, 2026 the US Department of War released its first batch of declassified UAP records, launching the public archive PURSUE, which the Trump administration says will be updated every few weeks. The release follows a February 19, 2026 directive from President Trump instructing federal agencies to identify, review and declassify documents related to extraterrestrial life and UAP, and includes dozens of agencies and tens of millions of records, many only on paper. The first tranche focuses on cases where the government cannot definitively determine the nature of the phenomena and contains Apollo 12 and 17 imagery, a cockpit transcript describing bright particles, FBI infrared images of unidentified objects over the western US in 2025, and military reports from incidents in the UAE, Greece, the Middle East, Africa, Japan, and North America. FBI Director Kash Patel said his agency handed over its first batch of classified UAP documents to the Pentagon‑led inter‑agency committee, while Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and DNI Tulsi Gabbard framed the release as evidence of the administration’s commitment to transparency and a joint declassification effort. The Department of War notes that many unresolved cases reflect insufficient data rather than anomalous capability, invites private‑sector analysis, and that the initiative’s success will depend on whether future tranches provide new operational detail rather than additional Apollo‑era curiosities and short infrared clips.
summaries.film-how-to-watch-sleeping-dog-online-st: Sleeping Dog, a documentary directed by Michael Lazovsky, follows journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell as he investigates UFO and UAP phenomena. The film draws on Corbell's personal archive of hundreds of hours of footage, including material from well‑known UFO figures such as George Knapp, John Lear and Bob Lazar. Lazovsky says the documentary serves as insurance for Corbell, who faces threats from rogue organizations and companies that allegedly try to silence him. Corbell has about 46 videos that have not yet been released to the public, and he hopes the White House and Congress will declassify more UFO footage. The film aims to humanize the subject, dispel conspiracy stereotypes, and encourage serious discussion, and it became available digitally on May 12.
summaries.translucent-beings-pastors-at-ufo-disclo: The U.S. Department of War released 162 UFO‑related files in the first tranche of the UFO Disclosure 2026, including classified NASA documents on UAP anomalies observed by Apollo astronauts such as Buzz Aldrin’s 1969 lunar landing sightings. U.S. Congressman Tim Burchett said the next set of UFO files would “blow laymen’s minds” and described it as “holy crap is coming.” Pastors Joseph Zupetz and Tony Merkel were briefed on May 8 2026 and reported that church leaders were shown images of translucent extraterrestrial entities with long limbs in wooded settings, which they said could fulfill a dark biblical prophecy. The pastors claimed that intelligence officials compared the disclosures to passages in Revelation and the Epistles of Paul about a “Great Deception” in the end times, and that God would send strong delusion to those who believe the lie. Other pastors, including Greg Locke, tweeted that the UFO discussion is a cover‑up for a demonic release and that a great deception is imminent as the age ends. The article notes that the interpretation of the evidence remains uncertain and that evangelical theories vary.
summaries.illusionist-uri-geller-reacts-to-us-cong: Israeli‑British illusionist Uri Geller, 79, reacted to US Congressman Tim Burchett’s warning that the next batch of declassified UFO files will dwarf those already released, saying he believes every word of the warning. Burchett, a member of the UAP Caucus, told Joe Rogan that the first set of 162 documents was only a “drop in the bucket” and that “Holy Crap is coming” with future releases. He cited reports of pilots being pulled from duty for psychological evaluation after UAP encounters, and alleged sightings of underwater craft moving over 200 mph and as large as a football field. Geller tweeted that in the 1970s he was shown fragments of metal from a crashed UFO by rocket scientist Wernher von Braun, and that he later saw non‑human bodies stored at NASA’s Goddard Space Center. He claims the already released files, which include Apollo and Gemini mission reports of strange lights and objects, validate his long‑standing assertions and hopes the forthcoming documents will expose NASA’s full involvement.
summaries.aliens-nephilim-boebert-weighs-in-on-uap: Last week the U.S. Department of War released previously classified documents on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena, with the White House, ODNI, DOE, AARO, NASA, FBI and other intelligence agencies involved, and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced the release as a step toward unprecedented transparency. Rep. Lauren Boebert discussed UAPs on the Breakthrough Hour podcast, citing biblical references to fallen angels and Nephilim and questioning a rumored Department of Defense project that might involve human genetics and hybrids, a line of inquiry she also raised during a 2024 House Oversight hearing. Colorado has a history of UAP activity, including 1970s cattle mutilations, 2020 drone sightings over fields near Senator Cory Gardner’s home, and a 2019 report of 30–50 drones with a larger mothership, but investigations have ruled out military involvement and left the origin and purpose of the drones unknown. The 2024 release included videos of orb‑like objects similar to the 2017 “Tic‑Tacs,” an eight‑pointed chandelier‑shaped UAP that may be a flare, and NASA Apollo 12 and Apollo 17 images and transcripts that show unexplained dots and objects near spacecraft, yet no definitive evidence of extraterrestrial life emerged. Public speculation remains high, with figures such as
summaries.a-small-oregon-town-puts-on-a-big-ufo-fe: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17, 2026, and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. The festival celebrates the 1950 UFO sighting by Paul and Evelyn Trent, whose photographs of a metallic object sparked national debate and are considered among the most famous UFO images. Organizers describe the event as a gathering where skeptics and believers meet, offering speakers, live entertainment, a parade, costume contests, a trivia session, and a costume ball. Ticketed speaker presentations are available through ufofest.com, while most other activities are free, and the schedule includes a “UAP pass pickup party,” a podcast recording, and a panel with an undisclosed whistleblower interviewed by Jeremy Corbell. The festival coincides with the release of previously unseen federal UFO files, adding new context to the town’s long‑standing UFO lore.
summaries.off-beat-federal-agents-an-eye-of-sauron: The U.S. Department of War posted 161 declassified UAP records to its PURSUE website after a February 19 Trump administration directive to open federal archives on unidentified aerial phenomena. Among the files is a 13‑page slide deck titled “Western US Event,” summarizing reports from seven federal law‑enforcement special agents (USPER1‑USPER7) who observed four distinct UAP incidents over two days in 2023 in the western United States. The incidents described include an orange orb that released smaller red orbs, a stationary glowing orange orb measured 12‑18 m across and 1,050 m away, a “dark kite” that behaved like a car but glided over the desert, and a “transparent kite” that caused a spotlight beam to stop and then resume. The Transparent Kite event lacks technical data such as radar or video, and the evidence relies solely on witness statements and cross‑team corroboration, leaving the nature of the object uncertain. The remaining 160 files comprise mission reports from Air Force and Navy operations (2020‑2024), FBI photo PDFs, sections of an old FBI “Flying Discs” administrative file, NASA Apollo transcripts, unresolved UAP summaries, and State Department cables, many of which were declassified in late 2025 or earlier. Secretary Pete Hegseth described the release as “unprecedented transparency,” and the web page includes a real‑time clock, coordinates set to the Pentagon, and a quote from President Trump.
summaries.ufo-files-in-pursuit-of-pies-in-the-sky: On May 8 the U.S. Department of War released 162 declassified documents, photos and videos on unidentified anomalous phenomena under President Donald Trump, with further releases planned through the PURSUE program. The release involves the White House, NASA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence and the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, and includes footage of UAPs over Greece, Iraq, the UAE and a football‑shaped object near Japan. Most of the files are heavily redacted—108 contain black masking and four were retracted—according to the Pentagon to protect eyewitness identities, facility locations and unrelated sensitive military information. AARO’s 2024 report added hundreds of new UAP sightings but found no confirmation of alien technology, and a second report is expected soon with low expectations for new revelations. The release has drawn criticism that it fuels speculation and that the Trump administration’s transparency is limited, while the site received over 340 million hits in the first half‑day, reaching more than 97 % of the U.S. population.

en.title: Bright lights and hot orbs: UFO files shed light on sightings but leave interpretation to the public
en.summary: On May 8, 2026 the US Department of War released its first batch of declassified UAP records, launching the public archive PURSUE, which the Trump administration says will be updated every few weeks. The release follows a February 19, 2026 directive from President Trump instructing federal agencies to identify, review and declassify documents related to extraterrestrial life and UAP, and includes dozens of agencies and tens of millions of records, many only on paper.

de.title: Helle Lichter und heiße Kugeln: UFO-Akten geben Aufschluss über Sichtungen, überlassen die Interpretation jedoch der Öffentlichkeit
de.summary: Am 8. Mai 2026 veröffentlichte das US-Kriegsministerium seinen ersten Stapel freigegebener UAP-Datensätze und startete damit das öffentliche Archiv PURSUE, das nach Angaben der Trump-Regierung alle paar Wochen aktualisiert wird. Die Veröffentlichung folgt einer Anweisung von Präsident Trump vom 19. Februar 2026, die Bundesbehörden anweist, Dokumente im Zusammenhang mit außerirdischem Leben und UAP zu identifizieren, zu überprüfen und freizugeben. Sie umfasst Dutzende von Behörden und Dutzende Millionen Aufzeichnungen, viele davon nur auf Papier.

en.title: How to watch 'Sleeping Dog' online – stream controversial UAP/UFO doc from anywhere
en.summary: Sleeping Dog, a documentary directed by Michael Lazovsky, follows journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell as he investigates UFO and UAP phenomena. The film draws on Corbell's personal archive of hundreds of hours of footage, including material from well‑known UFO figures such as George Knapp, John Lear and Bob Lazar. Lazovsky says the documentary serves as insurance for Corbell, who faces threats from rogue organizations and companies that allegedly try to silence him.

de.title: So schauen Sie sich „Sleeping Dog“ online an – streamen Sie die umstrittene UAP/UFO-Dokumentation von überall aus
de.summary: „Sleeping Dog“, ein Dokumentarfilm von Michael Lazovsky, begleitet den Journalisten und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell bei seiner Untersuchung von UFO- und UAP-Phänomenen. Der Film stützt sich auf Corbells persönliches Archiv mit Hunderten von Stunden Filmmaterial, darunter Material von bekannten UFO-Persönlichkeiten wie George Knapp, John Lear und Bob Lazar. Laut Lazovsky dient der Dokumentarfilm als Versicherung für Corbell, der Drohungen von Schurkenorganisationen und Unternehmen ausgesetzt ist, die angeblich versuchen, ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen.

en.summary: S. Department of War released 162 UFO‑related files in the first tranche of the UFO Disclosure 2026, including classified NASA documents on UAP anomalies observed by Apollo astronauts such as Buzz Aldrin’s 1969 lunar landing sightings. S.

de.title: Durchscheinende Wesen? Pastoren bei UFO Disclosure treffen auf gezeigte Bilder von Wesen, die eine dunkle biblische Prophezeiung ankündigen könnten

en.title: Illusionist Uri Geller Reacts To US Congressman's Warning Over Next Set Of UFO Files: 'Holy Crap Is Coming'
en.summary: Israeli‑British illusionist Uri Geller, 79, reacted to US Congressman Tim Burchett’s warning that the next batch of declassified UFO files will dwarf those already released, saying he believes every word of the warning. Burchett, a member of the UAP Caucus, told Joe Rogan that the first set of 162 documents was only a “drop in the bucket” and that “Holy Crap is coming” with future releases.

de.title: Der Illusionist Uri Geller reagiert auf die Warnung des US-Kongressabgeordneten vor der nächsten Reihe von UFO-Dateien: „Heiliger Mist kommt“
de.summary: Der israelisch-britische Illusionist Uri Geller, 79, reagierte auf die Warnung des US-Kongressabgeordneten Tim Burchett, dass die nächste Ladung freigegebener UFO-Dateien die bereits freigegebenen in den Schatten stellen werde, und sagte, er glaube jedes Wort der Warnung. Burchett, ein Mitglied des UAP Caucus, sagte Joe Rogan, dass der erste Satz von 162 Dokumenten nur ein „Tropfen auf den heißen Stein“ sei und dass mit zukünftigen Veröffentlichungen „heiliger Mist kommt“.

en.title: A small Oregon town puts on a big UFO festival. Why it's worth a visit
en.summary: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17, 2026, and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. The festival celebrates the 1950 UFO sighting by Paul and Evelyn Trent, whose photographs of a metallic object sparked national debate and are considered among the most famous UFO images.

de.title: Eine kleine Stadt in Oregon veranstaltet ein großes UFO-Festival. Warum es einen Besuch wert ist
de.summary: Das 26. jährliche McMenamins UFO-Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, findet vom 14. bis 17. Mai 2026 statt und wird vom McMenamins Hotel Oregon ausgerichtet. Damit ist es nach Roswell das zweitgrößte UFO-Festival in den Vereinigten Staaten. Das Festival feiert die UFO-Sichtung von Paul und Evelyn Trent im Jahr 1950, deren Fotografien eines metallischen Objekts eine landesweite Debatte auslösten und zu den berühmtesten UFO-Bildern zählen.

en.title: Off-Beat: Federal agents, an Eye of Sauron, and what's really in the Pentagon's first UAP drop
en.summary: S. Department of War posted 161 declassified UAP records to its PURSUE website after a February 19 Trump administration directive to open federal archives on unidentified aerial phenomena. Among the files is a 13‑page slide deck titled “Western US Event,” summarizing reports from seven federal law‑enforcement special agents (USPER1‑USPER7) who observed four distinct UAP incidents over two days in 2023 in the western United States.

de.title: Ausgefallen: Bundesagenten, ein Auge Saurons und was wirklich im ersten UAP-Abwurf des Pentagons enthalten ist
de.summary: Das US-Kriegsministerium veröffentlichte 161 freigegebene UAP-Aufzeichnungen auf seiner PURSUE-Website, nachdem die Trump-Regierung am 19. Februar angeordnet hatte, Bundesarchive zu nicht identifizierten Luftphänomenen zu öffnen. Unter den Akten befindet sich ein 13-seitiges Dia-Deck mit dem Titel „Western US Event“, das Berichte von sieben Spezialagenten der Strafverfolgungsbehörden des Bundes (USPER1–USPER7) zusammenfasst, die im Jahr 2023 an zwei Tagen vier verschiedene UAP-Vorfälle im Westen der USA beobachteten.

en.title: UFO files | In pursuit of pies in the sky
en.summary: S. Department of War released 162 declassified documents, photos and videos on unidentified anomalous phenomena under President Donald Trump, with further releases planned through the PURSUE program. The release involves the White House, NASA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence and the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, and includes footage of UAPs over Greece, Iraq, the UAE and a football‑shaped object near Japan.

de.title: UFO-Dateien | Auf der Suche nach Kuchen im Himmel
de.summary: Das US-Kriegsministerium veröffentlichte unter Präsident Donald Trump 162 freigegebene Dokumente, Fotos und Videos zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen. Weitere Veröffentlichungen sind im Rahmen des PURSUE-Programms geplant. An der Veröffentlichung sind das Weiße Haus, die NASA, das FBI, das Büro des Direktors des Nationalen Geheimdienstes und das All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office beteiligt. Sie umfasst Aufnahmen von UAPs über Griechenland, dem Irak, den Vereinigten Arabischen Emiraten und einem fußballförmigen Objekt in der Nähe von Japan.
